description.en: Delete the underlying shared memory block. This should be called only once per shared memory block regardless of the number of handles to it, even in other processes. unlink() and close() can be called in any order, but trying to access data inside a shared memory block after unlink() may result in memory access errors, depending on platform. This method has no effect on Windows, where the only way to delete a shared memory block is to close all handles.
